def: Agreed on by everyone, with no one against.
The easing of a burden; also official approval of accounts.
Firmly decided and unlikely to change.
Able to be seen or identified.
The state of being ill; a disease.
A rapid increase in the seriousness of a conflict.
The act of taking someone into police custody.
It is true that; admittedly.
Happening before the usual or expected time.
Feeling safe, protected, and cared for.
Calm and in control of one's feelings; also prepared for.
The things that are happening; the course of events.
Because of; owing to.
The state of being unable to pay one's debts.
The setting up of equipment; also the installed system.
An armed entry into and attack on another country.
A crash in which two things hit each other.
A local community with its own administration.
Acting firmly in line with one's principles or plan.
A large public gathering to show support or protest.
The dead body of a person.
The ability to move or travel freely.
Something that is needed and cannot be avoided.
To find the exact position of something.
More than enough; in generous amounts.
A woman who decides cases in a court of law.
Simple and unadorned; also just plainly so.
From that time up to now.
If; on the condition that.
Able to be felt or clearly noticed.
The act of making something stronger; also a refreshment.
Done at once, without delay.
The provision of a place to stay or store something.
In the time between; meanwhile.
To believe that someone may be guilty.
Occurring here and there; only in a few cases.
The cruel treatment of a group; also the act of chasing.
When compared with others; relatively.
The government minister in charge of the armed forces.
In advance; before anything else.
The pleasure of looking forward to something.
Something that a person plans to do.
Happening before the proper or expected time.
Yet again; one more time.
A payment demanded by an authority; also the handing over of something.
The act of refusing or turning something down.
The official ending of a law, custom, or system.
Coming at the end; final.
The level of the eyes; also equal standing between people.
The direction or focus something is set toward.
The act of setting someone or something free.
Able to be noticed or perceived.
The blocking of a place to stop goods or people getting in or out.
Energy and force that drives change or movement.
The ability to work well without wasting time or resources.
In the past; at an earlier time.
Strong anger at something seen as unfair or wrong.
The process by which something comes into being.
The building or setting up of something.
The moving of people away from a dangerous place.
The fact of being real or alive; also a livelihood.
An organised search, especially by police for a suspect.
The finishing of a piece of work or building.
Not certain; open to doubt.
The act of setting a prisoner free.
The chief official of a large organisation.
For the greatest part; mainly.
A punishment of being kept in prison.
An open area of land covered with rough grass and low shrubs.
To recognise and name who or what something is.
The speed at which prices are rising.
The state of standing for a job or election.
The amount that something can hold or produce.
Not any at all.
Not at all; in no way.
Going on steadily without a break.
A situation in which someone's life is at risk.
The part of a country's military that fights in the air.
Relating to or decided by the greater number.
The updating of something to make it modern.
The act of following on and taking over a role.
In a way that can clearly be seen.
Doubtful; not easily convinced.
A person who acts in place of another.
A raised structure of seats for spectators or speakers.
The spreading of something over a wide area.
To have something thanks to a person or cause.
An official rule made by an authority.
A careless error; something done by accident.
A court's judgement of guilt; also strong disapproval.
A pointer to something else; also an official warning.
In many different places.
In many ways or on many occasions.
A large party that draws support from across society.
Lasting only for a short time.
In opposition to; contrary to.
That cannot be avoided; forced or required.
Done quickly and without delay.
A state of confusion and disorder.
An emphatic 'yes', often used in reply to an order.
So silly as to deserve laughter or scorn.
Grand dignity; also a title for a king or queen.
Unwilling to change one's mind or ways.
A living creature; also the basic nature of something.
A title for royalty; also supreme authority.
Extra strength, support, or troops added to something.
Time without end.
A group of ships or vehicles under one command.
Something given; also a natural talent.
Faithful support for a person, group, or cause.
A wish for God's favour; also something that brings good.
A period during which an offender is watched instead of imprisoned.
Cruel and violent.
An official effort to find out the facts, especially of a crime.
Wonderful; extremely good.
Extremely interesting and attractive.
To allow something formally or politely.
A very sad event; also a serious play with an unhappy end.
To get or arrange something for someone.
A secret plan by a group to do something harmful or illegal.
The act of going down; also being moved to a lower league.
Suitable and fitting for the situation.
The state of being present in a place.
A group chosen to deal with a particular matter.
A means of escape from a difficult situation.
To ask someone questions to get information.
To say or do something that hurts someone's feelings or pride.
In the exact, real sense of the words.
A change produced by a cause; a result.
Caring only about oneself.
